# Can I AI?

> Benchmark and run on-device AI models directly in your browser. Test whether your device can run open-source LLMs (Gemma, Llama, Phi, SmolLM) locally via WebGPU and WASM, and see real-world performance from the community.

Can I AI is a multi-engine on-device AI platform. It detects your hardware, estimates VRAM, runs open-source language models locally in the browser using MediaPipe (WebGPU) with WebLLM and Transformers.js (WASM) fallbacks, and measures tokens-per-second so you can see what your device is actually capable of. Includes a chat interface, accuracy evals, multimodal (vision) support, voice in/out, and a Cloud-to-Client mode that streams a cloud model while a local model loads.

## Pages

- [Home](/): Device detection, hybrid inference engine, chat with on-device models, quick benchmarks, and the global FrugalFlops heat map.
- [Benchmarks](/benchmarks): Community benchmark feed with tokens-per-second, time-to-first-token, device fingerprinting, and per-model performance history.

## For AI agents

Can I AI exposes a public MCP (Model Context Protocol) server so coding/automation agents can run the same benchmark suite on their own host and compare results with the community.

- [MCP endpoint](https://gjovfujbfqwouopnupwb.supabase.co/functions/v1/mcp): Streamable HTTP MCP server. Tools: `list_benchmark_prompts`, `get_methodology`, `list_model_presets`, `get_community_benchmarks`, `submit_benchmark_run`.
- [MCP discovery](/.well-known/mcp.json): Machine-readable pointer to the MCP endpoint.
- [Benchmark prompts (JSON)](/api/benchmark-prompts.json): The 16-prompt suite (ttft, short, medium, long, reasoning, long-context, multi-turn, concurrent) for agents that don't speak MCP.
- [Methodology](/api/methodology.json): How tokens/sec, TTFT, TPOT and verdicts are computed.

## Optional

- [Lovable](https://lovable.dev): Platform this project is built on.
